source.definition: An official organization or body that has the power to enforce laws, regulations, or administer public affairs.

explanation.sourceText: The English word 'authority' comes from the Latin 'auctoritas', meaning influence, command, or power.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „authority” provine din latinescul 'auctoritas', care înseamnă influență, comandă sau putere.

target.definition: O organizație oficială care are atribuții administrative sau de control.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'autoritate' derives from the Latin 'auctoritas', meaning 'influence' or 'power'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „autoritate” provine din latinescul 'auctoritas', care înseamnă 'influență' sau 'putere'.

examples.sourceText: The authority has published the new rules.
I have to go to the authority to renew my passport.
examples.targetText: Trebuie să merg la autoritate să-mi prelungesc pașaportul.

examples.sourceText: The authority monitors compliance with the regulations.
Many administrative procedures are now possible online.
examples.targetText: Multe proceduri la autorități sunt astăzi posibile online.

learningTips.text: Remember 'authority' comes from Latin 'auctoritas', which means power or influence—just like the Romanian 'autoritate'.
The English word 'authority' sounds similar to Romanian 'autoritate', both referring to official power or organizations.
Think of 'authority' as the one who 'authorizes' rules and decisions—this helps link it to 'autoritate'.
Imagine a government 'authority' issuing laws; this will help you remember that 'autoritate' means an official body.
